title: Richly Charred Japanese Seafood Tendon
description: A charred, dense, one-pot Japanese dish featuring black cardamom pods and yakimono-grill, this tendon is a delicious and flavorful meal, friendly to those who avoid onion and garlic.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp and scallops to the skillet and cook until browned, about 3 minutes per side.
Add the black cardamom pods to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, sake, and mirin, then pour the mixture into the skillet.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the tempura bits to the skillet and cook until crispy, about 2 minutes.
Season the mixture with salt to taste.
Add the water to the skillet and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the seafood is cooked through and the sauce has thickened, about 10 minutes.
